title: The Architecture Landscape Recap
concept: We have covered GPT evolution, Mixture of Experts (MoE) routing, FlashAttention scaling, and multimodal image token projection.

exercises.prompt: What are we recapping in this final architectural landscape lesson?

options.text: How modern AI architectures combine routing, memory speedups, and multimodal inputs.
How to build a physical computer monitor.
How to write basic Python print statements.

exercises.explain: We have covered GPT history, Mixture of Experts (MoE), FlashAttention, and vision projectors. This recap brings them all together.

cards.title: Putting the Pieces Together
cards.body: We have traveled from basic text generators to massive multimodal systems. Let's step back and look at the big picture of how GPT history, routing experts, fast attention, and image translators fit together to build today's smartest AI systems!

cards.title: The Modern AI Landscape
cards.body: The modern AI landscape is a system of specialized components: GPT models set the foundational transformer architecture, Mixture of Experts (MoE) dynamically routes tasks to specialized sub-networks, FlashAttention speeds up attention calculations, and Projectors translate images into text-like tokens so the model can 'see' and 'read' at the same time.

cards.title: The Shipping Port Analogy
cards.body: Think of the architecture landscape like a modern shipping port. Instead of one giant crane struggling to lift every type of cargo, we have specialized teams (Experts) who only work when needed, faster conveyor belts (FlashAttention) that keep traffic moving without pileups, and translation docks (Projectors) that turn incoming boats (images) into the same shipping containers (tokens) that trucks (the LLM) already understand.

cards.title: AI in Action
cards.body: When you upload a photo of a recipe to a modern chatbot and ask for instructions, the model uses a Projector to turn the photo into visual tokens, FlashAttention to quickly read your long prompt, and MoE routing to send the cooking query to the 'culinary' and 'language' experts in its network.

cards.title: The Multimodal Myth
cards.body: Many think that adding vision capabilities means building an entirely new brain from scratch. In reality, we connect pre-trained vision brains to pre-trained language brains using a simple 'bridge' network called a projector, saving massive amounts of training time.

cards.title: Scaling vs. Modality
cards.body: Comparing how we handle attention scaling versus how we handle vision integration in modern architectures.

right.text: Translates visual features into text-like tokens for the LLM.

left.text: Optimizes memory reads/writes to speed up context windows.

exercises.prompt: How does FlashAttention speed up LLM processing?

options.text: It compresses text files before they are read.
It removes half of the layers in the neural network.
It optimizes memory access during the attention calculation.
It translates images into text descriptions.

exercises.explain: FlashAttention optimizes how the computer's memory is read and written during attention math, speeding up training and context scaling without losing accuracy.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Mixture of Experts (MoE) models must run every single parameter in the network for every token processed.
exercises.explain: Mixture of Experts (MoE) models only activate a subset of their total parameters (the selected experts) for any given token, keeping computational costs lower.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe how MoE models manage active parameters:
exercises.sentence: In MoE systems, a router handles the dynamic routing of tokens to specialized experts.
exercises.explain: Mixture of Experts (MoE) uses a router to send tokens only to the most relevant expert networks, saving computation.

exercises.prompt:
Identify the advanced multimodal AI system:
Model A: Receives an image, runs OCR to extract raw text, and feeds only that text into a basic GPT model.
Model B: Receives an image, runs it through a vision encoder, and projects the visual features directly into the text token space of the LLM.

options.text: Model A is the AI because it ignores the image and works faster.
Model B is the AI because it uses a projector to align image tokens with text tokens.
Both are traditional systems with no AI components.

exercises.explain: Model A represents an LLM that cannot handle images. Model B uses a projection layer to map image features into the LLM's text space, making it a multimodal system.

exercises.prompt: How does a multimodal projection layer bridge the gap between vision encoders and LLMs?

options.text: It translates the text query into a pixelated canvas for the image encoder to read.
It maps visual features into the same token embedding space used by the text model.
It bypasses the transformer completely to speed up image rendering.
It routes text queries to specialized image-generation experts.

exercises.explain: An image projection layer converts spatial image features into visual tokens that live in the same embedding space as text tokens, allowing the text-based transformer backbone to process them seamlessly.
